title: Mobile EEG split-belt walking study
description: This mobile brain-body imaging (MoBI) study investigates electrocortical correlates of locomotor adaptation during split-belt treadmill walking. Thirty participants performed both abrupt and gradual split-belt walking paradigms with a 2:1 belt speed ratio while mobile EEG was recorded, providing insights into neural mechanisms underlying gait adaptation. This dataset is a NEMAR publication of the OpenNeuro dataset (doi:10.18112/openneuro.ds004475).
methods_description: Mobile EEG was recorded during split-belt treadmill walking tasks. Participants (n=30) completed both abrupt and gradual split-belt walking conditions with a 2:1 belt speed ratio.
